Plain text field note (.txt)

This is a plain text file, not Markdown. BrainFrame opens .txt through the same
reader as Markdown, because Markdown is a superset of plain text. This paragraph
is hard-wrapped in the source into several short lines, and the renderer treats
each single newline as a soft break, so these lines reflow into one continuous
paragraph that fills the pane width.

This second paragraph is separated by a blank line, so it stays a distinct
paragraph rather than merging with the one above. If you see two paragraphs, the
first reflowed to the pane width, .txt rendering is working (plan case F6).

Because the reader parses this as Markdown, characters that mean something in
Markdown are still interpreted here — for example the next three lines become a
bulleted list:

- first item
- second item
- third item

Everything else is just ordinary text with no front matter and no headings.
